17979311.description:
Offering a harmonious balance of space, character, and modern living, the house is complemented by a marvellous west-facing garden measuring 55ft in width (at its widest point) X 37ft in depth, providing an expansive outdoor sanctuary. This superb garden is designed for both relaxation and entertainment, featuring a practical summer house benefitting from electricity and internet cabling and a covered outdoor seating area, perfect for barbecuing and enjoying the outdoors year-round. The size and orientation of the garden ensure it captures an abundance of natural sunlight throughout the day. The high rear wall of the garden affords exceptional privacy, making it a tranquil retreat for the whole family.
On the lower ground floor, the property accommodates a family kitchen which in turn provides access to a wine cellar and a storage vault. Moving through, the floor continues to a mixed use area equally suitable as a dining-room or an additional sitting -room with gas fireplace, and then to the light, main reception room (19' x 12') which itself leads into a modern conservatory, perfectly positioned to overlook the stunning garden.
The ground floor of the property consists of one reception room and a guest cloakroom with abundant built-in storage (formerly a full-bathroom, for easy re-conversion if desired). The first floor accommodates 2 generously sized double bedrooms. The second floor is home to a 3rd double bedroom offering additional storage and a light-filled atmosphere and the family bathroom which completes this level.
This wonderful residence blends traditional Georgian architecture with contemporary design, making it the perfect home for those seeking spacious living, modern amenities, and a beautifully private outdoor space. Cloudesley Road is ideally located to take advantage of the area's excellent amenities. The vibrant Islington district is just a short distance away, offering a wide array of local shops, cafes, restaurants, and bars to suit all tastes. The renowned Upper Street is easily accessible, offering high-end boutiques, stylish dining options, entertainment and cultural experiences. Families will appreciate the proximity to well-regarded local schools, and the surrounding area boasts a variety of green spaces, including Barnard Park, Islington Green, Thornhill Gardens and Thornhill Square, perfect for leisurely strolls or family outings. Transport links are excellent, ensuring easy access to central London and beyond. The property is within close proximity to Angel Underground Station (Northern Line), offering quick and direct access to the City and the West End. Additionally, Highbury & Islington Station (Victoria Line and National Rail), providing excellent connectivity across London. For those who prefer to travel by bus, numerous routes run nearby, offering further convenience. Whether you're commuting into the city or enjoying the local area, Cloudesley Road provides the perfect balance of tranquillity, convenience, and connectivity.

17886516.description:
Nestled in the heart of Angel on the peaceful and sought-after Bromfield Street, this beautifully preserved three-bedroom Grade II listed freehold house offers an exceptional blend of historic charm and modern convenience. Spanning approximately 1,845 square feet and boasting a generous 42-foot rear garden, this elegant Georgian home is a rare find in one of Islington's most desirable enclaves.
As you enter via the raised ground floor, you're welcomed into a stunning double reception room, bathed in natural light thanks to its dual aspect outlook over tranquil Bromfield Street and the expansive garden. Period details are beautifully preserved, including original fireplaces, exposed wooden floorboards, and working shutters, lending an air of timeless sophistication. On the lower half landing, you'll find a well-placed guest cloakroom and direct access to the garden—perfect for entertaining or relaxing outdoors. The lower ground floor opens into a spacious open-plan kitchen and dining area, featuring a separate utility room and access to the front patio and vaults, offering ample storage and functionality. The first floor comprises a generously proportioned principal bedroom (15' x 13') with views over the quiet street, complemented by a large four-piece family bathroom to the rear. On the second floor, two further double bedrooms both benefit from built-in wardrobes, while a mezzanine-level shower room serves the upper floor with style and convenience. Set within the Barnsbury Conservation Area, Bromfield Street is a picturesque, tree-lined street known for its peaceful atmosphere and elegant period homes. Despite its quiet charm, it sits just moments away from the vibrant energy of Upper Street, with its diverse array of independent boutiques, cafes, gastropubs, restaurants, and art-house cinemas. Connectivity is excellent, with Angel Underground Station (Northern Line) within close proximity, providing direct access to the City and West End. Highbury & Islington Station (Victoria Line, London Overground, and National Rail) is also nearby, offering quick links across London and beyond. Numerous bus routes run along Upper Street and Liverpool Road, making commuting and getting around London both simple and efficient. This elegant period home on Bromfield Street represents a rare opportunity to own a substantial, character-filled house in the heart of one of London's most vibrant and well-connected neighbourhoods.

18943080.description:
A charming Victorian home situated on the ever popular Albion Terrace.

Description

This charming home opens into a welcoming entrance area with practical storage for coats, creating an inviting first impression. Throughout the home you will find hardwood flooring, original fireplaces, beautiful sash windows and useful built-in storage, all contributing to the warmth and character of this attractive property.

To the front of the ground floor is a generous reception room with excellent proportions, ideal for both relaxing and entertaining. A second versatile room provides flexibility to suit a range of needs, whether as an additional reception, guest bedroom, home office, or playroom. To the rear, a bright open-plan kitchen and dining room forms the heart of the home, offering a sociable space for everyday living with direct access on to a private garden perfect for indoor-outdoor entertaining.

Upstairs, there are two well-proportioned bedrooms arranged around a central landing, along with a family bathroom.

Blending period charm with practical modern living, this Victorian home offers excellent flexibility, attractive original features, and a private garden, making it an ideal choice for a wide range of buyers.

Location

Albion Terrace is a charming residential street located within one of Hackneys most desirable postcodes, positioned adjacent to the sought-after Albion Square. Commissioned in 1844 by Sir William Middleton, the square is noted for its historic design, which architectural historian Nikolaus Pevsner described the square as being "clearly influenced" by the celebrated landscape designer and writer John Claudius Loudon. Its central garden saved from demolition remains an attractive ornamental space with symmetrical planting, mature trees, lawns, and a picturesque pavilion for residents to enjoy.

The surrounding area offers an excellent selection of local amenities, with a variety of independent cafés, restaurants, and pubs nearby, particularly around the vibrant De Beauvoir and Dalston neighbourhoods. The green open spaces of London Fields are approximately 0.6 miles away, alongside the popular Broadway Market, known for its weekend food stalls, shops, and community atmosphere.

Transport connections are convenient, with Haggerston Station approximately 0.3 miles away and Dalston Junction Station around 0.6 miles away, providing easy access to the City, Canary Wharf, and beyond. Numerous bus routes also serve the area, offering additional connectivity across London.

All times and distances are approximate. Source: googlemaps.com.

Square Footage: 1,025 sq ft

The ever changing Kingsland Road now offers a wonderful range of independent shops, cafes and restaurants.

Chantry Street is a quiet location, situated in the heart of Angel. Upper Street is a quick stroll from your front door, as well Angel (Northern Line) Underground. Regent's Canal is also within easy reach. You are spoilt for choice with an array of pubs, cafes, shops and local eateries.

18759886.description:
Handsome Victorian House, close to Victoria Park

Description

Tucked away on a peaceful residential cul-de-sac between London Fields and Victoria Park, this handsome four-storey Victorian house sits on a sought-after corner plot and is offered in excellent condition throughout. Rich in original period features and natural light, the home combines character, space and comfort, making it an ideal family home.

The accommodation is thoughtfully arranged and currently comprises a welcoming reception room, a generous kitchen/diner ideal for everyday family life and entertaining, three to four well-proportioned bedrooms, a family bathroom and shower room on the top floor. A charming patio garden to the rear can be accessed from both the ground and lower ground floors, providing flexible outdoor space perfect for children, dining and relaxed family living.

Location

Warneford Street is located within 0.5 miles of Victoria Park Village which boasts a Ginger Pig Butcher, Gail's Bakery, delicatessen, fishmonger, grocery stores, and a selection of popular pubs and restaurants.

The beautiful green spaces of Victoria Park (0.2 miles) and London Fields (0.5 miles) are both nearby with their gardens and play areas. There is also a superb assortment of pubs, cafes and shops on Broadway Market, Hackney Road and Mare Street.

Transport links can be found at London Fields station (0.4 miles) and Bethnal Green tube (0.8 miles) along with frequent buses to Shoreditch and the City, as well as Highbury and Islington.

Square Footage: 1,808 sq ft

15220340.description:
CSG has this breathtaking double fronted home on one of De Beauvoir’s most desirable streets. The freehold is currently arranged as two units, with the main space arranged over the upper levels and a separate apartment, with a large garden and separate side access on the lower level. Accommodation has been considered and recently renovated, comprising; the majority of the space within the main house, with two double bedrooms and a bathroom (en-suite) on the raised level. There is a spacious kitchen/dining space set on the first floor, with an additional two double bedrooms on the top floor. There is an oversized one bedroom apartment housed on the lower floor, benefitting from its own private entrance which is accessed via the side of the main house, also providing access to courtyard garden.


The closest stations are Dalston/Kingsland & Dalston junction, which forms part of the London overground. For those wishing to travel by bus, Kingsland Road and Southgate Road provide a number of good routes, with the delights of vibrant, trendy Hoxton and Shoreditch just a short journey away, as are the National Rail and Victoria line trains of Highbury & Islington. The lovely green spaces of De Beauvoir Square and London fields are a short stroll away.


A must view.

18258566.description:
A rare opportunity to refurbish a late Victorian home in a lovely residential road.

Description

Battledean Road is a quiet late Victorian residential road tucked behind Highbury Fields. The house is neither Grade listed or within a Local Conservation Area. The house has been in the same ownership for approximately 60 years and is now ready for some improvement. The orientation of the house is east at the front enjoying morning light, the rear garden faces west for the evening sun. The rooms have many period features retained and the house offers the opportunities to create a fantastic family sized home in a preferred location.

After refurbishment the house could offer:
• Four double bedrooms
• Two generous bathrooms
• WC
• Outside WC
• Or alternatively five bedrooms, one bathroom and separate WC
• Drawing Room
• Further reception room
• Fabulous family sized kitchen diner leading out to:
• West facing rear garden

Location

Just behind Highbury Fields this Battledean Road is very well located for the excellent amenities on the Fields including tennis courts, the leisure centre and 33 acres of park. Highbury Corner is just 0.4 miles (Google) with it’s fantastic transport links including bus routes, London Overground, National Rail and the Victoria Line providing access to all directions.

Square Footage: 1,786 sq ft

18831543.description:
Perfectly positioned on the ever-popular Plimsoll Road, N4, this mid-terraced Victorian four double bedroom, two-bathroom family home spans over 1,500 sq ft and beautifully combines period character with modern comfort. The property benefits from a well-sized garden and enjoys an excellent location moments from the amenities, cafés and transport links of Finsbury Park and Highbury Barn.

Set on the sought-after Plimsoll Road, N4, this impressive mid-terraced Victorian family home offers over 1,560 sq ft of beautifully balanced living space across three floors.

Arranged over three floors, this property offers exceptional entertaining space, including a stunning double reception room with high ceilings, ornate cornicing, feature fireplaces and a beautiful bay window, leading through to a bright and well-appointed kitchen/dining room overlooking the garden.

To the rear, a landscaped garden provides an ideal setting for outdoor entertaining and family living.

The upper floors comprise four generously proportioned double bedrooms and two bathrooms, including a spacious principal bedroom, offering flexible accommodation for growing families or those working from home.

Further benefits include a cellar for storage, excellent natural light throughout and scope to extend (STPP), if desired.

Plimsoll Road is superbly positioned for the vibrant amenities of Finsbury Park and Highbury, offering an excellent array of cafés, restaurants, independent boutiques and green spaces. Outstanding transport connections are available via Finsbury Park Station (Victoria, Piccadilly and National Rail), along with Arsenal Station (Piccadilly Line) and Crouch Hill Overground. The property is also within the catchment for a number of highly regarded local schools, making it an ideal choice for families.

13737019.description: A characterful Victorian terrace which offers potential to extend and improve (STPP), set on a peaceful terrace of five houses, only moments from ever evolving Newington Green. There is an additional bedroom/ study and a W.C at the rear of this level. The lower level houses a double bedroom at the front, with the kitchen/dining space set at the rear, leading to the conservatory which provides access to the secluded rear garden. There are two double bedrooms and the family bathroom suite positioned on the top floor. A rare opportunity that must be viewed to be appreciated. Leconfield Road is a quiet residential street, perfectly located for local shops, cafes and restaurants situated nearby in Newington Green, Highbury Barn and Stoke Newington which are all well known for their village atmosphere can also be found locally and provides a selection of boutique shops and bars as well as theatres and a vibrant nightlife. The green spaces of Highbury Fields and Clissold Park are within easy reach, as well as newly refurbished kids playgrounds in Newington Green and Butterfield Park. Transport across London is made easy with Overground links at nearby Canonbury, providing easy access to the City and East London, whilst Highbury and Islington (Victoria line) is the closest underground link. Numerous good bus routes can also be found nearby along with easy access to St Pancras International, the City and links to Stansted Airport. The City is within cycling distance.
features: Set on a peaceful terrace of only five houses

18840963.description:
*Guide Price £1,750,000 - £1,900,000*
A substantial five double bedroom Victorian home measuring approximately 1,950 sq. ft., positioned in the heart of Victoria Park Village and offered chain free.

Thanks to its position, the house enjoys open aspects on both sides, allowing natural light to flow through while taking in attractive views to the front and rear.

Arranged over three principal floors plus cellar, the house offers a highly practical layout, with all primary living space positioned at ground level rather than below, and well-balanced bedroom accommodation above. The ground floor centres around an impressive double reception with high ceilings, sumptuous wood flooring, sash windows and retained period detailing, leading through to a spacious kitchen/dining room with direct access to both the courtyard garden and a separate patio area, ideal for everyday living and entertaining.

The first floor provides three genuine double bedrooms and a family bathroom, while the top floor comprises of two further double bedrooms and an additional bathroom. The configuration creates a natural separation between living and sleeping spaces, making it particularly well suited to modern family life.

To the rear, the private courtyard garden enjoys an calming outlook towards the church. Victoria Park and the open green spaces of Well Street Common are just moments away, offering extensive outdoor space close to home.

18967156.description:
Enviably positioned on one of Stoke Newington’s most sought-after residential streets, this substantial Victorian family home offers well-proportioned accommodation arranged over three floors.

The house retains a number of attractive period features and enjoys excellent natural light throughout. The ground floor comprises a welcoming entrance hallway leading to a bright and elegant reception room featuring a charming cast-iron fireplace, high ceilings and generous proportions. To the rear, a separate kitchen provides ample storage and worktop space, with doors opening directly onto a beautifully secluded south-facing garden — an ideal setting for outdoor dining, entertaining and family use, benefiting from sunlight throughout the day.

The upper floors provide a generous principal bedroom, along with three further well-proportioned double bedrooms. A family bathroom and additional shower room serve the accommodation, offering flexibility for growing families.

Belgrade Road is a quiet residential turning ideally located within easy walking distance of Stoke Newington Road, Church Street, Newington Green and Dalston. The area offers a diverse mix of boutique shops, independent cafés and award-winning bars and restaurants, giving it a vibrant yet community-led feel.

Transport connections are excellent, with Dalston Junction and Rectory Road Overground stations both within easy reach, providing swift routes to Liverpool Street and across East London. 18863496.description:
Beautifully refurbished and impeccably styled, this impressive four-bedroom period terrace offers spacious, versatile living across four floors on a sought-after residential road.

Packed with original character, from high ceilings and ornate cornicing to hardwood floors and cast iron radiators, it’s been thoughtfully updated with quality modern finishes including underfloor heating, double-glazed hardwood sash windows with shutters, and bespoke fitted storage throughout.

The ground floor offers a welcoming hallway with WC and two bright reception rooms, both featuring marble fireplaces and a wood burner in the front lounge.

Downstairs, the stunning open-plan kitchen/diner is flooded with natural light from two large skylights and full-width bi-fold doors opening onto the private garden. It boasts polished concrete flooring, bespoke hardwood cabinetry, a central island, range cooker and excellent storage.

The basement also provides a cinema/reception room with projector, built-in bar and wine cooler, plus a separate utility room.

Upstairs are three generous bedrooms and a stylish family bathroom with bath and walk-in shower, while the top floor hosts a fourth bedroom with vaulted ceiling and en-suite shower room.

A fantastic mix of period charm and contemporary living.

Barrett's Grove is a tree-lined residential street that sits within catchment of outstanding Shacklewell Primary School. Butterfield Green is just at the end of the road, making a popular picnic and exercise spot in the warmer months, and the eclectic mix of independent shops, bars and restaurants surrounding Dalston and along Stokey High Street are all just a short walk away. Connections into the City and Central London are swift and simple, too, with Rectory Road Station and Dalston Kingsland (both Overground) within easy reach.


EPC Rating: C

15926526.description:
Nestled just moments away from Victoria Park in E9, this exquisite family home is a luxurious end-of-terrace property spanning three floors. Boasting a charming private garden, a rooftop terrace, and underfloor heating throughout, this residence offers a blend of comfort and sophistication.

The Property
Meticulously renovated and extended by the current owner, the house spans over 1,500 sqft of thoughtfully designed internal space. With 4 bedrooms, 3 bathrooms, and a dedicated utility/laundry room, every detail has been carefully considered. The secure garage, spacious enough for a modern family car, adds practicality to this elegant home. Situated at the end of the road with neighbours on only one side, it provides a serene and private living environment.

The main entrance has been repositioned to the raised ground floor, set nicely back from the road. This leads you into the primary entertaining space, with a modern, open plan kitchen and broad living area. Large windows provide a wealth of natural light, and the raised ground setting make it a peaceful & private haven in which to relax.

The floor below is perfect for your children’s bedrooms, or housing guests. It features two double bedrooms, each with their own bathroom and direct access to the garden. Here you’ll find the utility area, and direct access to the garage from the house. Windows throughout give a bright feel, making the area feel like it’s own separated living space.

The first floor hosts the main bedroom with an ensuite bathroom, a separate wardrobe area, and access to the roof terrace. Enjoy sweeping views while sipping your morning coffee or unwinding with an evening drink as the sun sets.

This is the perfect property for families, with its clever layout and separation of bedrooms. It would be equally attractive for those seeking to entertain, with friends and guests able to socialise across the spacious garden, the roof terrace, and the large open plan kitchen and living area.

Additionally, the property holds significant potential for expansion or the addition of a separate dwelling (subject to necessary approvals), with previous consent granted for such development.

The Local Area
Situated near Victoria Park, London's oldest public park, residents can enjoy the famous Sunday market, offering a variety of pop-up eateries, coffee bars, craft beers, and fresh produce stalls. With over 200 acres of green space bordered by picturesque canals, “Vicky Park” is perfect for leisurely strolls, jogs, or relaxing with a good book.

Nearby Well Street and Lauriston Road provide a diverse range of eateries & dining options, from French and Mediterranean to Caribbean and Indian cuisine. Explore local favourites such as the picturesque Well Street Kitchen, brunch spot Boulangerie Jade, or gastropub The Empress. Alongside these are convenient amenities like Whole Foods Organic store and the outstanding Ginger Pig butchers.

If you’re looking for slightly more fast-paced entertainment, Hackney & London Fields are also walkable. There you’ll find an energetic scene with pubs, bars, cinemas, and wide-range of events and activities.

The Transport
Three stations are within easy walking distance: London Fields, Hackney Central & Homerton, as well as accessible bus routes (26, 106) to central London. The property ensures easy connectivity, wherever you’re going.

Come and explore this wonderful home, which seamlessly combines contemporary comfort, within a highly sought after neighbourhood.

A separate utility room is also located on this floor, adding further practicality. The first floor comprises two generous double bedrooms and two bathrooms, one of which is en-suite. 17350459.description:
Converted Victorian shopfront to an extraordinary three-storey house

Description

This is an exceptional residential freehold house carved from an early Victorian shopfront that had once been a thriving fishmonger, the late Georgian building spent time as an antiques shop and a cartography gallery before its conversion to a residential property.

Uha Global Architects, being award-winning, have done an exceptional job designing this property and it has been styled with first rate skill to create the impressive living space.

High quality materials have been used throughout including bespoke joinery, underfloor heating, integrated Miele appliances and the bathrooms have Boffi and Duravit fixtures. The three floors offer very flexible living space with up to three bedrooms if required. Light and the utility of the space has been utilised through-out to create a most appealing residence in the Barnsbury Conservation area.

The ground floor has light and airy living space, a large shower room WC and a bedroom/home office. The stunning kitchen with natural walnut veneer and white matt lacquer units has masses of storage and a cleverly designed built-in dining area.

The first floor front is arranged as a living space with both storage and impressive bookcases. The rear is occupied by the main bedroom, again with plenty of storage space, with it’s naturally lit en-suite bathroom. Across this floor is a wrap-around skylight that stretches from the back of the property across the roof.

The open staircase leads to the top floor which is currently arranged as an office but could be considered a third bedroom if required. This room is off of the roof terrace and has a Miele wine fridge. Steps lead up to the rooftop that is very private yet enjoys views. There is seating, storage and a built-in BBQ.

A truly beautiful home that would suit many buyers who appreciate and recognise quality design and build.

Location

In the heart of the Barnsbury Conservation area Thornhill Road is well placed for the travel arrangements including buses, undergound, London Overground, National and International rail links at The Angel, Highbury Corner and a little further Kings Cross. The many amenities on Upper Street and central Islington are accessible.

Square Footage: 1,126 sq ft

18943269.description:

The property is set within a secure gated courtyard development just off Islington Park Street. 18930360.description:
A spacious, five double bedroom family house positioned on a quiet tree lined street in Highbury, N5. Standing at a very impressive 2,150 sqft, the property offers a wealth of character along with being presented to the market on a chain free basis. Accommodation comprises of a wonderfully bright double reception room on the raised ground floor, with a well-equipped kitchen towards the rear. The first floor occupies three good sized double bedrooms, and a family sized bathroom, while a cleverly extended loft conversion houses the master bedroom and a further ensuite. The lower ground of the building would make a perfect rental, given it’s currently set up as a separate apartment, though the building remains under one title. A sizeable east facing garden completes the property and should be viewed at your earliest convenience.
Leconfield Road is located within a short distance of the open green spaces of Highbury Fields and the prestigious Highbury Barn. Clissold Park is found at the end of Petherton Road, with a lido, small zoo and playground it has something for everyone. It is also just moments from the fantastic restaurants, pubs and shops of the vibrant Stoke Newington Church Street. The property is also close to Canonbury Overground, taking you straight into the City, west, east and south London. Highbury and Islington tube (Victoria Line) and Arsenal tube (Piccadilly Line) are both close to the property as are numerous bus routes taking you to the City, London Bridge and Angel.

18852355.description:
This substantial 5 bedroom property offers space, light and a desirable location in the heart of Newington Green. Featuring a large garden and terrace, the fireplaces and original wood flooring ready for restoration.

Burma Road is only a short walk from the local amenities on Newington Green. For open spaces and recreation you can find West Reservoir and Clissold Park nearby. The nearest station is Canonbury (Underground and National Rail).

18980783.description:
Set on the charming and sought-after Linton Street, this elegant three-bedroom, three-bathroom four-storey period home offers beautifully balanced living space, a west-facing garden in the sought-after Arlington Square Conservation Area.
You enter on the raised ground floor, where a wonderfully proportioned double reception room immediately impresses. Bathed in natural light, the space enjoys leafy views over the quiet residential street to the front and across the private west-facing garden to the rear perfect for late afternoon and evening sun. On the half landing down towards the lower ground floor, a versatile study provides an ideal work-from-home space, with direct access to the garden a peaceful and private outdoor retreat. The lower ground floor is arranged as an open-plan double room comprising a generous dining area and well-appointed kitchen, creating an excellent space for both everyday family life and entertaining. Ascending through the house, the half landing up to the first floor accommodates a well-finished family bathroom. The first floor itself is dedicated to a superb principal bedroom suite, complete with built-in wardrobes and a Jack and Jill bathroom. The top floor provides two further double bedrooms, both with built-in wardrobes, alongside a separate shower room ideal for family members or guests.
Linton Street is a particularly attractive and peaceful residential turning in the heart of Islington, characterised by handsome period façades and a strong sense of community. Despite its tranquillity, the house is moments from the vibrant amenities of both Upper Street and Essex Road. Upper Street offers an exceptional selection of boutiques, independent shops, restaurants, cafés and bars, as well as well-known brands. Nearby highlights include the renowned Almeida Theatre and a wide choice of gastropubs and eateries. Essex Road provides further local conveniences, supermarkets and neighbourhood cafés. Excellent transport links are within easy reach, with Highbury & Islington station (Victoria Line & Overground), Angel station (Northern Line), and Essex Road station offering swift access to the City, the West End and beyond. Numerous bus routes along Upper Street further enhance connectivity. Combining period character, generous accommodation and an outstanding central Islington location, this is a superb family home in one of North London's most desirable settings.

18798118.description:
A bright and characterful three-bedroom, two-bathroom period house, with garden, situated in the heart of Barnsbury and within the Barnsbury Conservation area.

Accommodation is well balanced and neatly presented comprising; a grand entrance hallway, leading to the dual reception space which occupies the raised ground floor, complete with ornate cornicing and fireplace, flooded in natural light courtesy of the dual aspect and large sash windows. The lower-level houses the spacious fitted modern kitchen/dining room which provides access to the low maintenance rear and front garden. The two double bedrooms on the top floor offer fitted wardrobes with the third bedroom perfect as a study or a guest bedroom. The two-family bathrooms, one with bathtub, are located on the respective landing areas. The property is complete with under the stairs utility room accessed via the kitchen.

Located moments from Barnsbury Wood, Hemingford Road provides superb access to the transport at Highbury & Islington Station (National Rail & Victoria Line) providing excellent links around London; the London Overground with Caledonian Road and Barnsbury Station; and Caledonian Road Station (Piccadilly Line) with trains through the West End out to Heathrow. The buzz of Upper Street is only a short walk away. The celebrated gastropubs the Duchess of Kent, the Albion and the Drapers Arms can be found locally within Barnsbury, and the incredibly popular restaurant/café Sunday is situated just up the road. Supermarkets Waitrose and Sainsbury's are located at the Southern end of Liverpool Road, close to Angel. In addition, the transport hub of Kings Cross/ St Pancras International lies at the Southern end of Caledonian Road. The new Kings Cross regeneration/ development which includes Granary Square, new restaurants and shops, and a brand new Waitrose, are within close proximity.

18812577.description:
*Guide price £1,250,000 - £1,350,000*
A substantial four double bedroom Victorian freehold offering over 1,600 sq. ft. of well-proportioned accommodation, set on a no-through residential turning on the border of Stoke Newington and Dalston.

The house blends original character with modern updates, retaining features such as sash windows, exposed brickwork and wooden flooring, while benefiting from a thoughtfully extended rear.

The ground floor provides a generous double-through reception room and dining area. The extended kitchen/diner has been thoughtfully designed as an entertaining space with a central island, seating area and bi-fold doors opening onto a secluded patio garden, creating an easy flow for that inside/outside feel. Sky lights allow for a wealth of natural light to flood through. A guest cloakroom and access to a storage basement completes this level.

Arranged over the upper floors are four genuine double bedrooms. The first floor comprises of two bedrooms with a striking family bathroom with two further bedrooms on the top floor, both with en-suite facilities, offering a practical layout suited to modern households.

John Campbell Road is ideally positioned for access to the independent cafés, restaurants and local shops of Stoke Newington Church Street and Dalston. Clissold Park is within walking distance, while Canonbury, Dalston Junction and Dalston Kingsland Overground stations provide swift connections across London. Numerous bus routes from nearby Newington Green offer direct access to the City and West End.

Offered with no onward chain.

Tenure: Freehold
Council Tax band: E
Local Authority: Hackney

12632133.description:
A fabulous four bedroom Victorian house arranged over three levels and situated in a leafy residential road close to Stoke Newington Church Street, Abney Park and Clissold Park.

The property is spacious and beautifully presented throughout with many charming original features including period fireplace, stripped wooden floorboards, sash windows and high ceilings with ornate period ceiling detail. The entrance hallway serves the reception and dining rooms, the kitchen / diner and provides access down to a cellar that is used for storage.

The property is wider than average, boasting approximately 1599 sq ft of internal accommodation comprising on the ground floor of a spacious and bright reception room with bay window to the front. Featuring a magnificent period cast iron fireplace, stripped wooden floorboards, picture rail and high ceiling with exquisite original coving and ceiling rose. There is a dining room to the rear, with tiled floor, and sash window overlooking the garden, with coving and picture rail.

There is a bright dual aspect kitchen/diner to the rear of the ground floor with window to the side, vaulted ceiling with skylight and bi folding doors opening out onto the garden. The kitchen is fitted with a range of recently installed white, high gloss, contemporary units incorporating integrated appliances, benefiting from stylish brick tiled splashback, wooden floorboards and spotlights to ceiling. With vertical radiators and wooden burning stove. The good sized patio garden is surrounded by raised brick built flower beds.

On the upper levels there are four bedrooms, a family bathroom and guest WC.

There is an exceptionally spacious double master bedroom, with two sash windows providing lots of natural light, boasting high ceiling with original coving and picture rail. The second bedroom is also a generous double and benefits from sash window providing leafy garden views.

There is a wonderful family bathroom, fitted with a three piece suite comprising of bath with overhead shower and glass screen, vanity wash hand basin and WC. With stylish geometric tiled splashback and floor tiles, sash window and original ceiling detail.

On the lower half landing there is a smaller double bedroom featuring built-in alcove wardrobe, picture rail and sash window to the rear. There is also a guest WC on this level.

On the top floor there is another good sized double bedroom with sash window overlooking gardens to the rear.

Alkham Road is situated close to Stoke Newington Church Street offering a fantastic range of trendy independent boutique shops, pubs, coffee shops and restaurants. The neighbourhood is quiet and leafy, but also close enough to the buzz of Stoke Newington Church Street. There are also excellent local shops and amenities along Stoke Newington High Street and Stamford Hill.

Close to excellent transport links including Stoke Newington overground station, which offers a fast and frequent service into Liverpool Street in one direction and Seven Sisters for connections to the Victoria Line in the other. There are also excellent local bus routes into the City and West End and the 106 bus affords direct access to Finsbury Park Station.

For recreation the property is within easy reach of Abney Park, Clissold Park and Stoke Newington Common in one direction and Hackney Marshes, Springfield Park and the River Lea in the other. The West Reservoir and Climbing Centre and the acclaimed Woodberry Wetlands are also a short distance away.

18742652.description:
A rare opportunity to acquire a substantial four-bedroom late Victorian family home on one of Stoke Newington’s most sought-after residential crescents. This charming property oozes character and offers an exciting blank canvas for those looking to create their perfect London home.

The Victorians started developing the area in 1850 after the rector of St Mary’s Church granted a lease of 11 ½ acres of land to a local builder to build 21 houses close to Clissold Park. Slowly these expanded to form Clissold Crescent (originally named Park Lane) aimed at wealthy middle-class families.

Set back from the street on this desirable, leafy residential road, the property enjoys an uninterrupted elevated position with great kerb appeal, and sits conveniently close to the vibrant high street amenities of Stoke Newington’s Church Street and open green spaces of Clissold Park.

Offering almost 1,700 sq ft of internal living space, the house currently provides flexible accommodation with potential to reconfigure into a statement family residence or retain the separate self-contained apartments (subject to planning) within the freehold building or separating the leases and disposing individually.

The ground floor welcomes you with bright, light-filled hallway with stairs to the floor above and a cellar below. The reception (4th bedroom) is to the right and leads through to the separate dining room with galley kitchen directly off of it and leading seamlessly out into a south-facing private rear garden. There is potential under permitted development to extend the rear of the property creating a large open-plan kitchen diner further extending the floor area by another 200-300 square feet subject to planning permission.

The first floor provides a well-balanced layout centred around a generous principal bedroom measuring approximately 17’9” x 15’8”, with a bay window that enhances natural light and sense of scale. A well-proportioned kitchen sits to the rear, offering ample workspace and storage, while a contemporary shower room is positioned conveniently off the landing. The arrangement works well for flexible living, whether as a private suite, guest accommodation or an additional reception level.

The second floor comprises two further double bedrooms, including a particularly spacious room of around 17’10” x 12’5”, complemented by a third bedroom ideal as a child’s room, study or dressing room. A separate kitchen and additional shower room serve this level, creating excellent versatility for multi-generational living, rental potential or independent accommodation, all thoughtfully arranged off a central staircase landing.

With full renovation required, this home presents a rare chance to tailor the layout, design and finishes to your own exacting standards. Sold with vacant possession and with no onward chain, this is a superb opportunity for both owner-occupiers and investors to acquire a substantial property with minimal hassle.

Perfectly positioned for transport and lifestyle, local stations including Canonbury, Drayton Park and Arsenal Underground are all within walking distance, while excellent schools, cafés, shops and park are just moments away.
features: To be sold with vacant possession
Potential for ground floor extension STPP

18740846.description:
A STUNNING SOUGHT AFTER LOCATION FREEHOLD MAISONETTE OVER THREE FLOORS.

A beautifully presented three bedroom Victorian maisonette with a south facing private garden with parking. Set over three storey's.

Saint Marks Rise, is a quiet residential street running between Dalston and London Fields. The park and lido are within walking distance as well as popular Wilton Way, Ridley Road & Broadway Markets. Hackney Downs, Hackney Central are less than half a mile away offering easy access into the City and the rest of London.

Offers Over Three Double Bedrooms Maisonette Victorian Conversion South Facing Terrace & Garden

18977245.description:
This bright and well proportioned four bedroom terraced house is presented in excellent condition throughout and features a beautifully maintained rear garden and fabulous master bedroom leading to sunny roof terrace.

This bright and well proportioned four bedroom four storey house is presented in excellent condition throughout and features a beautifully maintained private west-facing rear garden and fabulous master bedroom leading to sunny roof terrace. The property also comprises elegant reception room, conservatory-style kitchen/breakfast room, master bedroom with roof terrace and en suite shower room, a further three good-sized bedrooms (one could possibly be a second reception room) and bathroom. De Beauvoir Road is located in De Beauvoir Town and this lovely family home is within easy reach of the superb range of shops, bars and restaurants of trendy Upper Street while providing motorists with quick and easy access to the A1 road links out of London and to the North. The nearest station is Dalston Kingsland (East London Line) with Highbury & Islington (Victoria Line, National Rail) also close at hand which supply a swift journey into the City and West End.

De Beauvoir Town is within easy reach of the superb range of shops, bars and restaurants of trendy Upper Street with great transport links including Dalston Kingsland and Highbury & Islington.

18674356.description:
Highly original four bedroom modern house set within an exclusive gated development located moments from Church Street and Clissold Park.
Just one of four this unique property is arranged over three floors, offering wonderfully spacious, stylish and contemporary living right in the heart of Stoke Newington. The accommodation comprises on the ground floor a stunning entrance hall with glazed floor to ceiling windows enclosing an inner courtyard, and two double bedrooms, one with a luxury wetroom with bath and ingenious skylight, both with built-in storage and external folding doors. There is an additional guest shower room on this level. On the first floor a generous semi-open-plan living room with huge picture window and engineered oak flooring opens through to a separate and well-appointed modern kitchen; from here sliding glass doors lead out to the decked terrace, with glass balustrade, looking out over the developments central courtyard. The top floor offers two further bedrooms, including a glorious master bedroom with large picture window giving a fantastic southerly aspect towards the City. Both rooms feature skylights, built-in wardrobes and ensuite facilities.
Favourably situated for the areas many amenities, including popular local schools, The Quarters four houses are arranged around a calm and tranquil cobbled courtyard complete with benches and mature pot plants. Leafy Clissold Park is just nearby and Church Street's independent shops, eateries, pubs and coffee bars are just a few steps away. There are excellent transport connections to the City and West End, with numerous bus routes, and trains to Liverpool Street from Stoke Newington Station (Overground).
Available Chain Free.

18399153.description:
A Grade II listed, four-storey home with three to four bedrooms, two bathrooms, a separate WC, a reception room, and parking. Completed in 1846, Keystone Crescent is celebrated as the smallest crescent in Europe. The houses are uniform in design, featuring round-arched doorways, Welsh slate roofs, and a distinctive stucco band at second-storey level.

Keystone Crescent sits just north of King's Cross, offering a quiet residential enclave of elegant Victorian terraces. Moments away are the new Google headquarters and Coal Drops Yard, placing world-class workplaces, shopping, dining, and transport connections within easy reach.

Tenure: Freehold
Council Tax Band: F

Northdown Street sits on a peaceful, community‑focused street in King’s Cross, offering a blend of neighbourhood charm and excellent proximity to the area’s vibrant amenities and transport links.
Kings cross station/St Pancras is just 300 meters from the house, offering multiple links in and out of London (Underground services/National Rail/International Eurostar)

18416889.description:
A characterful and charming four storey Georgian townhouse within close proximity to Upper Street. At nearly 1300 sq ft of internal living space, the property comprises a large kitchen with an adjacent dining room on the lower ground floor. There is access to a utility room and a door leading to a 30 ft private, mature garden. There is a double aspect, a double reception room on the raised ground floor which is flooded with natural night and features a fireplace. Additionally, a conveniently placed W.C. is adjacent to this room. The principal bedroom is located on the first floor, benefiting from two sash windows and fitted wardrobes. The large modern bathroom is situated on this floor and offers a separate shower and bathtub. There are two further double bedrooms on the second floor completing this well proportioned house. The ceilings have been vaulted in the second bedroom exposing the wooden beams exuding more character within this cosy home.


The house affords convenient access to the artisan cafes, shops, bars and eateries, with particular note to those on Upper Street and Exmouth Market, a short walk away. The delights of Camden Passage are within a stone's throw, giving a plethora of options to potential purchasers. Angel Underground (Northern Line) provides convenient access to both the City and the transport hub that is Kings Cross / St Pancras International, with bus routes on St John Street, Upper Street, Pentonville Road and City Road.

18451568.description: A fabulous 3 double bedroom house set within this private gated mews development situated just off Arlington Avenue and fronting the Regents Canal. Delightful for its seclusion in a wonderful tucked away position, accommodation comprises 3 double bedrooms (principal bedroom with en-suite shower room) and the main bathroom on the ground floor, on the first floor there is a guest WC, and a fantastic full width reception room with exposed brick and 2 large windows overlooking the canal, the reception is open to the kitchen. From the reception room a spiral staircase rises to the top floor which opens to a roof top bedroom/ study with doors opening to the private roof terrace. The roof terrace is absolutely stunning, affording views across London and down the Regents Canal. There is off street parking within the development and a secured pedestrian gate providing access from the development directly to the canal footpath. The house sits within the Arlington conservation area, only moments from the gardens of Arlington Square with the many amenities of Islington Green, Upper Street, Angel and Broadway Market are just a short walk away, with the Canal towpath providing a wonderful tranquil passage. A plethora of transportation links encompassing many bus routes to the City and West End are available from both New North Road and Essex Road, with Essex Road National Rail station just a short distance away. The fashionable bars, restaurants, galleries and boutique shops of Hoxton, Shoreditch, London Fields and Old Street are all easily accessible from this desirable location.

18393643.description:

18183059.description:
Traditional 3 bedroom terraced house offering specials interiors, family bathroom, separate kitchen with breakfast area, dining room, charming reception room and a private back garden.

18416707.description:
Available chain free and situated on a quiet residential street, this well-proportioned three bedroom Victorian home offers over 1,000 sq ft of bright and versatile living space along with a south facing garden, two bathrooms and excellent potential to extend.

The ground floor features two spacious reception rooms with high ceilings, bay window and wooden flooring, creating an ideal layout for both relaxation and entertaining. The fitted kitchen provides generous worktop space and opens directly onto the private south facing garden, giving the home a natural flow and plenty of light. A large family bathroom with both bath and separate shower completes this level, along with access to a useful basement storage area.

Upstairs are three double bedrooms, each benefiting from large sash windows that bring in plenty of natural light. The principal bedroom spans the full width of the property and a second shower room adds valuable convenience.

The garden is part paved and part lawned with mature planting and enjoys sun throughout the day. The property also offers excellent scope for further enlargement, including the possibility of a loft conversion or a side return kitchen extension, subject to the usual planning consents.

Prince George Road is ideally positioned for the cafés, shops and restaurants of Stoke Newington Church Street, Newington Green and Dalston, as well as the wide open spaces of Clissold Park. Transport links include Dalston Kingsland, Dalston Junction and Rectory Road stations, with multiple bus routes into The City and West End.

18680380.description:
Fabulous five bedroom Victorian terraced house located directly opposite leafy Stoke Newington Common, moments from Church Street and popular local schools.
This impressive family home is arranged over four floors, boasting period features, and wonderfully light & well-proportioned rooms throughout. The accommodation comprises a generous reception room with bay window, shutters and original fireplace; large kitchen-diner with bespoke marble-topped units and integrated appliances; utility room; modern bathroom; stylish shower room, and five beautiful double bedrooms, three with built-in wardrobes. A delightful landscaped rear garden, well-stocked with trees and shrubs, extends to 65' (approx), offering a favourable southerly and unusually private aspect.
Conveniently positioned a few steps from Church Street and the High Street's eclectic array of independent shops, eateries, pubs and coffee bars, houses on the Common come on the market very rarely. There are excellent transport links to the City and West End, with numerous good bus routes, and trains to Liverpool Street from stations at nearby Rectory Road (Overground) or Stoke Newington (Overground).

18666422.description:
A stunning three/four bedroom freehold family home with off-street parking, ideally located just a short walk from London Fields. This is a fantastic opportunity to secure a home in one of East London’s most desirable locations.

Flooded with natural light throughout, the ground floor boasts a sleek open-plan kitchen with contemporary fittings, generous dining space and direct access to a secluded private garden, perfect for entertaining. A separate study and guest WC complete this level. The first floor offers a bright and airy reception room featuring a charming fireplace and Juliette balcony, alongside a well-proportioned double bedroom and a luxurious family bathroom suite. The second floor comprises two spacious bedrooms with integrated storage, including a principal bedroom with en-suite facilities. Further benefits include excellent storage throughout the property.

Perfectly positioned to enjoy the vibrant atmosphere of East London, this house is a must-see for those seeking contemporary living in a prime location. Middleton Road is just moments from the vibrant Broadway Market, renowned for its eclectic mix of restaurants, gastro pubs and independent boutiques. The expansive green spaces of London Fields are also nearby, perfect for leisurely walks or outdoor activities. This sought-after East London address offers easy access to trendy neighbourhoods such as Dalston, Stoke Newington Church Street, and De Beauvoir Town – each brimming with local charm and a wealth of amenities.

Commuting is effortless, with Haggerston Overground Station just a short walk away, offering swift connections to Shoreditch, Islington, Canary Wharf, London City, and Whitechapel (via the Elizabeth Line). Multiple frequent bus routes also provide convenient travel across the capital.

18358789.description:
Contemporary townhouse in London Fields with secure off-street parking.

Description

A beautifully presented three-storey townhouse offering approximately 1,343 sq ft of thoughtfully designed accommodation, complete with a private garden and balcony. Developed as a live/work property, it provides exceptional flexibility for modern living, seamlessly blending residential comfort with space for working from home.
The ground floor features a welcoming entrance hall leading to a spacious open-plan kitchen and reception area, ideal for entertaining or everyday family life. Full height double doors open directly onto a private garden, creating a wonderful connection between indoor and outdoor spaces. A W/C completes this level.
The first floor comprises three versatile rooms, currently arranged as bedrooms and a dressing room, but equally suited to home offices or creative studios.
The principal bedroom and family bathroom occupies the entire top floor, offering a peaceful retreat with direct access to a private balcony. Useful additional storage can also be found in the roof space.
This property combines contemporary design with practical versatility, making it an excellent choice for those seeking a stylish home in the heart of East London with the added benefit of a share of freehold and an allocated parking space.

Location

1321492.description: This enchanting, beautifully presented Victorian townhouse is a rare find, located on a desirable, quiet pathway in sought after Hackney Central. It provides exceptional, versatile accommodation arranged over four floors and enjoys a host of characterful features including original fireplaces and a large easily accessible loft storage space. It offers endless charm; comprising light entrance hall, stylish kitchen/diner opening to a cosy living/ entertaining space with access to a wonderfully bright conservatory, five delightful bedrooms, a glorious bathroom and a convenient shower/utility room. Moments from vibrant Mare Street and all amenities, many transport links including Hackney Central, Hackney Downs overground stations. Close to Broadway Market, the Lido, and the popular green spaces of London Fields. Catchment area for a choice of Outstanding Primary and Secondary schools.
features: Safe Cycle Path to London Fields

14476078.description: Accommodation is well proportioned and benefits from a rarely found courtyard entrance, providing useful external storage and extra security. The entrance provides access to the beautiful original staircase, with kitchen and dining rooms housed on the raised ground floor. The kitchen features a fabulous original ceiling from 1840, with original ceiling rose and coving. The dining space is situated at the front of the raised level, complete with marble fireplace, polished wooden floors and flooded in natural light courtesy of double glazed south and west facing windows. A spacious double reception room occupies the lower level, with a partition that divides the space if required. Ample built in storage and a wood burning stove further enhance this living space. Accessible from this level is a larger than average, secluded garden with patio and further benefitting from street access. A family bathroom is found on this level, with a further bathroom upstairs. There are two large double bedrooms on the first floor, which enjoy wider than average space given the plot the house sits on as it fans out toward the rear. The front bedroom is the larger and enjoys an impressive and unobstructed tree filled view over the garden through two large sash windows. Given the position on the street, the bedroom is not overlooked. A third bedroom/study space can be found on the top floor. Beautifully reimagined and meticulously refurbished, this exceptional four-bedroom Victorian home spans approximately 1,830 sq ft across four floors and combines elegant period detailing with refined contemporary finishes. Every element has been upgraded with premium materials and craftsmanship, including brand-new timber sash windows, a bespoke kitchen with porcelain travertine worktops, Smeg appliances, a 4-in-1 kitchen tap, and a striking Crittall glass wall that enhances natural light throughout. The home features engineered dark oak chevron flooring, underfloor heating across the ground and lower-ground floors, electric UFH in all bathrooms, luxurious Mandarin Stone and Claybrook tiles, Aquaroc bathroom fittings, and brand-new carpets across the upper levels. Set moments from the vibrant village atmosphere of Newington Green—and just 0.2 miles from Canonbury Station and 0.5 miles from Dalston Kingsland—the property offers a rare blend of character, craftsmanship and modern luxury in a highly connected N1 location.

THE PROPERTY

The lower ground floor has been transformed into a spectacular open-plan bespoke kitchen and dining room, featuring porcelain travertine worktops, Smeg appliances, a 4-in-1 boiling/filtered tap, and a striking Crittall-style glass wall that floods the space with natural light. Floor-to-ceiling Cortizo sliding doors open directly onto the landscaped rear garden, creating a seamless indoor–outdoor feel. This level benefits from engineered dark-oak chevron flooring, underfloor heating, a separate WC, and a well-proportioned guest bedroom.

On the ground floor, two elegant reception rooms—each retaining beautiful period panelling and cornicing—offer versatile living and entertaining spaces. The rooms are separated yet connected through the home’s thoughtful layout, with soft natural light enhanced by the newly installed timber sash windows.

The first floor comprises two generous double bedrooms, including a refined principal suite with a stylish en-suite bathroom finished with Mandarin Stone and Claybrook tiles, complemented by Aquaroc fittings and electric underfloor heating. A further modern family bathroom sits off the landing.

The top floor hosts a spacious loft bedroom with excellent ceiling height and flexibility—ideal as a guest suite, home office or studio space.
Throughout the property, every detail has been carefully considered: brand-new carpets, expertly restored period features, and a fully integrated Mistek fire-suppression misting system for added safety. The home’s mechanical upgrades include underfloor heating on both the ground and lower-ground floors and electric UFH in all bathrooms.

Externally, the property benefits from a front driveway and residential permit parking available through the local council (Council Tax Band E).

This is an immaculate turnkey home on one of N1’s most desirable streets—perfect for buyers seeking style, substance and exceptional craftsmanship in a prime Islington location.

THE LOCATION

Newington Green Road is superbly positioned in one of North London’s most vibrant and characterful neighbourhoods, just moments from the historic Newington Green. The area offers a true village feel, with leafy streets and a strong sense of community, while still benefiting from excellent city connections. Canonbury Overground Station is a short walk away, and frequent buses — including the 73 and 141 — provide quick access to the West End, the City and surrounding districts.

The immediate area is known for its independent shops and lively food scene. Around Newington Green you’ll find artisanal bakeries, organic grocers, boutique stores, cafés and popular restaurants such as Jolene, Primeur and Oliveta. This mix of everyday essentials and stylish eateries gives the neighbourhood a warm, creative and highly sought-after atmosphere.

Green space is close at hand, with Newington Green Gardens on the doorstep and the expansive Clissold Park a short stroll away — both ideal for relaxation, exercise and family time.
Families also benefit from strong local schooling. Newington Green Primary School, nearby, is well regarded for its ambitious curriculum and supportive environment, while City of London Academy, Highbury Grove, offers a good secondary option within easy reach.

Altogether, the location combines convenience, character and community — making it a highly desirable setting in the heart of N1.

Do not miss your opportunity to see this amazing property. Call us now!

Offering approximately 1,448 square feet of living space, the property boasts four generously sized bedrooms, a front and rear garden, and an abundance of storage throughout.

Designed with both comfort and practicality in mind, the ground floor features a large eat-in kitchen, perfect for entertaining, alongside a convenient separate W/C. Ascending to the first floor, a welcoming reception room provides an ideal space for relaxation, complemented by a well-proportioned bedroom. The second floor hosts two double bedrooms, both fitted with wardrobes for effortless storage, and a family bathroom. At the pinnacle of the home, the third floor showcases a fourth bedroom complete with a fitted shower and wardrobes, as well as an additional separate W/C.

Well maintained yet offering scope for refurbishment, this substantial property represents an exceptional canvas for future enhancements. Its prime location in one of North London’s most sought-after neighborhoods ensures easy access to an array of independent boutiques, acclaimed restaurants, and excellent transport links, making it an outstanding choice for discerning buyers.

Brett Close is moments from Stoke Newington Church Street which is the centre of the boutique shops, brilliant restaurants and pubs, local Fishmonger and Butcher, coffee shops and bars, that this area is renowned for. The Library, post office, bakery, wine shop, optician, dentist, pharmacy, doctor, local shops are all nearby. Clissold Park is a short stroll away.

There are excellent transport links including numerous bus routes along Green Lanes, Albion Road and Stoke Newington Church Street. There are numerous train stations in the immediate vicinity including Manor House and Arsenal Tube Stations, Canonbury, Dalston and Rectory Road over ground stations and Finsbury Park.

For recreation the flat is opposite Clissold Park, with its playground, tennis courts, paddling pool, Skate Park, animal enclosures and Clissold House. The Clissold Leisure Centre is just around the corner and the Shakespeare walk adventure playground at Butterfield Green and the West Reservoir and Castle Climbing Centre are also a short distance away.

16671265.description:
Welcome to this charming and spacious five-bedroom Victorian terraced house, ideally located in a sought-after residential area. Spanning multiple floors, this property offers a perfect blend of classic architecture and modern convenience, with a total gross internal floor area of approximately 2,084 square feet (193.63 square meters).

Upon entering, you are greeted by a striking entrance hall featuring original Victorian tiled flooring in a geometric pattern, complemented by a beautifully preserved wooden staircase with intricate balustrades. The ground floor boasts a generous reception room with hardwood floors, a decorative fireplace, and large bay windows that flood the space with natural light. Adjacent to this, the dining room provides an elegant setting for entertaining, also featuring a fireplace and access to the kitchen. The kitchen is well-equipped with modern appliances, wooden cabinetry, and tiled splashbacks, opening onto a light-filled conservatory that leads to the rear garden.

Upstairs, the first and second floors house five well-proportioned bedrooms, each with unique character and ample space. The master bedroom, located on the first floor, offers generous proportions and a tranquil atmosphere. There are also two bathrooms, including a family bathroom and an additional WC, ensuring convenience for all residents. The property includes a lower ground floor with additional living space, perfect for a home office, playroom, or extra reception area.

The exterior of the property is equally appealing, with a well-maintained front garden and a private rear garden featuring a gravel patio, lush greenery, and mature plants, ideal for outdoor relaxation or gardening. Situated on a quiet street, this home is close to local amenities, excellent schools, and transport links, making it an ideal family residence. With its period features, such as high ceilings, cornicing, and sash windows, combined with modern updates, this house offers a rare opportunity to own a piece of history with contemporary living comforts.

Original Victorian tiled flooring and wooden staircase in entrance hall.

18096794.description:
Freehold house comprising of five self contained bedsits.

Description

A freehold house configured as five separate studio apartments.

Each apartment has been thoughtfully refurbished with a modern bathroom suite, fully fitted kitchen and a bright reception room/bedroom space. The lower ground floor unit has its own entrance and opens out to a private courtyard to the rear of the building.

The building is offered chain free and ready for immediate occupation.

Please note, the whole building is for sale, and each individual studio apartment has it's own EPC D rating.

Location

Lorenzo Street enjoys a prime location in King’s Cross, one of London’s most vibrant and best-connected neighbourhoods. The area has been transformed through extensive regeneration, now home to a thriving mix of shopping, dining, and cultural destinations.

Just 0.3 miles away, Coal Drops Yard offers over fifty boutique shops, cafés, bars, and restaurants set around beautifully restored Victorian architecture, while nearby Granary Square (0.4 miles) provides open-air fountains, event spaces, and canal-side seating. The London Canal Museum and Camley Street Natural Park, both within half a mile, offer unique local attractions celebrating the area’s history and green spaces.

Transport connections are exceptional, with King’s Cross St Pancras Station (0.25 miles) provides access to six Underground lines (Circle, Hammersmith & City, Metropolitan, Northern, Piccadilly, and Victoria) offering quick travel across central London and direct links to Heathrow Airport and the West End.

St Pancras International (0.35 miles) connects to destinations across the UK and Europe via the Eurostar, while Angel Underground Station (0.5 miles) provides additional Northern Line access to Islington and the City.

16692503.description:
The result of a collaboration between Flawk and Nikjoo, this exquisite three-bedroom house in Stoke Newington cuts a striking profile. Soaring ceilings and generous panes of glass create bright yet inviting interiors, while bespoke detailing - including undulating bath screens and a steel wire balustrade - speaks to the attention to detail employed throughout. The house, known as Lode, has two separate outdoor spaces: a ground-floor courtyard garden and a private roof terrace.

The Designer

Founded by Bartlett graduate Ashley Law in 2022, Flawk is a purposefully small-scale property developer transforming under-loved sites. The projects are characterised by tactile and high-quality materials, as well as bespoke fixtures and objects that bridge artistic appeal and functionality. Operating at the intersection of design and culture, Flawk often collaborate with artists, designers and craftspeople to create homes peppered with bespoke - often playful - details.

The Tour

Clean lines define Lode's profile, which is formed of red brick and aluminium-framed windows. Secure gates lead to a sheltered entrance way, which draws the eye towards Douglas fir beams. The courtyard garden can be reached from here.

Ahead is the front door, which opens onto a bright lobby area with an excellent provision of coat and shoe storage. The WC, which has a sink made from dark-veined reclaimed marble, also has a secret hatch with an under-stair store.

The generous and open living spaces occupy the ground floor, their walls painted in Pointing by Farrow and Ball. East-facing, the room receives a soothing morning light through its Douglas fir-framed front windows. Delicate linen curtains can be drawn for privacy, with sculptural metal rings custom designed by Flawk.

Made from brushed steel, a fully integrated and bespoke kitchen stretches the breadth of the left-hand wall. Tactile round handles add softness, while an exposed breezeblock wall (which also extends to the upper levels) adds textural contrast against the smooth oak floorboards underfoot. Special attention has been given to sourcing the best amenities: the fridge is by Bosch, oven and microwave by Smeg, dishwasher by Miele and there is a Neff induction hob. A sliding pocket door from the entrance hall effectively delineates the space.

The first two steps of the staircase are made from concrete and stainless steel - a clever nod to the adjacent room - while the remaining stairs are oak. Thin steel wire forms the balustrade, which reveals itself on the top-floor landing, alongside a pendant lamp by James Shaw.

Two large double bedrooms with en suite bathrooms lies on first floor, alongside a useful utility cupboard. Both rooms have full-height cabinetry with bespoke, pink-hued cherry wood doors. One is presently used as a study; its en suite has a large marble-lined bath with a step-in shower and an undulating bath screen by Flawk.

A large skylight in the second bedroom draws light deep into the space. The adjoining en suite has a shower with a bespoke chainmail shower curtain. Wall lights are by Alexandra Arias.

The main bedroom sits on the top floor, with double glazed doors that open directly onto the terrace. Exposed white-painted beams amplify the light that pours in through two aspects. There is an additional en suite, separated via a walk-in wardrobe.

Outdoor Space

A concrete-tiled courtyard garden lies at the rear, with evergreen ferns and palms that burst from Cor-Ten planters around the perimeter. The space provides a shadier respite to sit during the warmer months and has been carefully laid with stone tile.

By contrast, the decked roof terrace is something of a sun trap; an open window cut into the brick frames long views across Stoke Newington. East-to-west facing, it receives day-long natural sunlight.

﻿The Area

The house is in the centre of Stoke Newington, with its cafés, restaurants, pubs, bars and shops. Much-loved Church Street is minutes away and is home to The Spence Bakery, Rasa, The Good Egg and Escocesa; there is also a branch of Whole Foods. The Jolly Butchers pub is at the end of Garnham Street and is something of a local institution, specialising in craft beer and excellent pub food. Sonora Taqueria, on Stoke Newington High Street is famed for its Mexican food. Cosy pub The Axe is a few minutes' walk towards Stoke Newington Common and does a delicious Sunday roast. Neighbourhood favourite, Esters, is only a 7 minute walk away and serves excellent coffee.

The house is within easy walking distance of peaceful Abney Cemetery and Park, one of London's 'Magnificent Seven'. The greenery of Clissold Park lies just beyond, with its tennis court and family of deer. A charming coffee stop, Fink's Pump House, is conveniently placed for a weekend morning's stroll.

Popular with young families, there are great schooling options in the immediate area. There are two excellent primary schools on Dunsmure Road: Holmleigh Primary School and Sir Thomas Abney.

The house is almost equidistant from Stoke Newington and Rectory Road stations; both run Weaver line services to Liverpool Street or to Seven Sisters for Victoria line connections. Numerous buses run into central London and the neighbouring Dalston area.

Council Tax Band: TBC

18958632.description:
A truly sensational, four double bedroom house positioned moments from the tube on one of the finest streets in N5, Highbury Hill. Standing at an impressive 2,300 sqft, the property has been tastefully decorated and extended throughout making it an ideal family home. Accommodation comprises of a wonderfully bright double reception room with a gorgeous feature fireplace, solid wood flooring and shutters in the bay windows. The rear of the house is breathtaking; a stunning open plan kitchen area with plentiful cupboards, sociable island with a breakfast bar, and bifolding doors opening out to a magnificent garden, finished to the rear with a cleverly designed outbuilding – perfect as an office or gym. The first floor is quite traditional with a beautiful bedroom to the front spanning the full width of the building, two further doubles and a family sized bathroom. The loft has been converted to create a master floor, with ensuite and double doors with peaceful views across neighbouring gardens. The property is completed with a downstairs wc, and a useful, sizeable cellar.
Highbury Hill is set moments from the iconic clocktower at the entrance to Highbury Fields and is perfectly set for the independent shops at Highbury Barn. The high street shops, bars and restaurants on Upper Street are located via the vibrant open spaces of Highbury fields.
Transport links are some of the best around creating effortless access across London. Arsenal offers the closest underground (0.1 miles) links on the Piccadilly line whilst Highbury and Islington provides the Victoria line and overground services. The weekday service from Drayton Park goes to Moorgate and an array of bus routes are on hand which provide routes to Angel, The City and West End.

18374633.description:
Overlooking Clissold Park, this charming and superbly positioned Victorian house offers an exceptional opportunity to create a beautiful home in one of Stoke Newington’s most desirable locations. The layout comprises a wider-than-average bay-fronted double reception room with home office area, a spacious kitchen and dining room, three double bedrooms including a full-width principal bedroom with views over the park, a family bathroom, separate WC and a full-width cellar providing useful storage. To the rear is an approximately 49–50 ft private garden, offering excellent potential for landscaping or future extension (subject to usual consents), there is also the benefit of an external storage room which has the washing machine and dryer.

The house retains a number of attractive period features including high ceilings, sash windows, cornicing and original fireplaces. Its front-facing rooms enjoy panoramic outlooks across the open green spaces of Clissold Park.

Queen Elizabeth’s Walk is a quiet, tree-lined street regarded as one of Stoke Newington’s most sought-after addresses, with the park quite literally on the doorstep. Excellent transport links include Stoke Newington Station (Overground) and various direct bus routes into The City and West End, as well as the independent shops, cafés and restaurants of Church Street and Newington Green all within easy reach.
