18980491.description:
Nestled 13 meters back from the street, at the end of a professionally landscaped front garden, this mid-century home has been thoughtfully extended and refurbished with an array of carefully chosen contemporary additions creating a tranquil light-filled series of beautifully resolved spaces.

The property features slimline Velfac Windows favoured by architects for their exceptional thermal and acoustic performance and outstanding high security credentials.

At the heart of the home, the open-plan kitchen, dining area and delightful garden sitting room form an outstanding living space. Bespoke glass-fronted oak cabinetry, a seamless Corian worktops and sink, integrated NEFF appliances, and a concealed tea/coffee/drinks station create a highly functional and refined kitchen. The VELFAC floor-to-ceiling sliding doors create an impressive, indoor/outdoor living environment with seamless level access to the stunning rear garden with outdoor power, accent wall lights, mature olive tree, tree ferns and fa custom fitted 2-meter granite topped BBQ station and storage - perfect for entertaining. The rear gate gives access to, a private off-street parking space, additional outdoor storage, bike rack and secure gated driveway. Both the rear and front garden feature automatic irrigation systems.

Inside, lighting is carefully curated, dimmable throughout, including Tom Chung and Jordan Murphy pendants for Audo Copenhagen and multiple Flos Glo-Ball fittings. Sustainable strand-woven bamboo flooring, chosen for its natural warmth, and durability, remote-control blinds, an Ultrasilent ceiling fan, downstairs WC, Knightsbridge flush-fitted sockets with USB charging points and discreetly housed utilities, add to the sense of considered practicality.

The spacious main sitting room features custom wall-to-wall, ceiling-height curtains, while enhanced sound insulation along the party wall and a purpose-designed air gap between neighbouring properties ensure exceptional acoustic comfort.

Upstairs, with wall to wall curtains and wool carpets throughout, the first and second floors offer two bedrooms each and two beautifully appointed Bathrooms featuring Lusso Stone fittings, Mandarin Stone terrazzo tiles, a deep soaking bath and 2 large walk-in showers. The generous 1st floor hallway has three metres of full-height built-in wardrobes and a bright dedicated home-office space. Top floor rooms have bespoke fitted wardrobes and access to a large under-eaves storage space.

18995323.description:
*Guide price £1,500,000 - £1,750,000*
A substantial four double bedroom Victorian home overlooking Stoke Newington Common and offering over 2,100 sq. ft. of well-proportioned accommodation.

Positioned directly opposite the Common, the house enjoys an unusually open outlook to the front, allowing natural light to flood through and creating a bright, airy feel rarely found on residential streets. To the rear, the garden is private and secluded, providing a peaceful outdoor space away from the city.

The ground floor begins with a double-through reception room featuring high ceilings, a bay window with shutters, a marble fireplace with wood burner and exposed wooden flooring. To the rear, the house has already been extended to create a generous additional living area currently arranged to suit accessible ground-floor use, with a bathroom alongside. The extension means the space could also be reconfigured to form a large open-plan kitchen and dining area opening onto the garden, if desired.

Upstairs, the first floor hosts a spacious principal bedroom with leafy views, alongside a further double bedroom and a large family bathroom with both bath and shower. The second floor provides two additional double bedrooms, while there remains potential to extend further into the loft (subject to the usual consents).

The rear garden extends to approximately 46 ft., mainly laid to lawn with a decked seating area, offering a quiet and private setting for outdoor dining or relaxing. There is access to a garage, ideal for storage that is currently rented for a yearly fee.

18995737.description:

The ground floor comprises an entrance hall, a spacious kitchen dining room measuring 15'6 by 14'9 ft and a reception room measuring 14'9 by 14'2 ft with doors leading directly to a private rear garden extending to approximately 32 ft.

The first floor offers a generous principal bedroom measuring 14'9 by 8'6 ft, two further double bedrooms both measuring 11'4 by 8'4 ft, a family bathroom and ample storage.

19005669.description:
Nestled in a quiet location, this stunning double-fronted three-bedroom period home has been sympathetically restored to an exceptional standard, blending timeless elegance with modern comforts. Local legend suggests this unique row of homes was originally built to house retired Royal Army Officers, in the 19th Century.

Each of the three generously proportioned bedrooms boasts original feature fireplaces, classic sash windows, and beautifully finished wooden flooring, exuding character and charm. The master bedroom offers a rare bonus - an access hatch to the flat roof, ideal for summer ventilation and ease of maintenance (a previous owner kept bee hives up here!).

The family bathroom is presented in a crisp white suite, adorned with sleek vertical tiling, sash and Velux windows, and mirrored cabinetry that enhances light and space. A separate ground floor W/C adds convenience. A large rooflight over the central stairway creates a light and airy hallway - also perfect for looking at the stars at night.

At the heart of the home is the expansive eat-in designer kitchen/diner - a true showpiece. Featuring a striking central fireplace, stylish tiled flooring, and a modern island with induction hob, suspended extractor fan and double Neff oven, it is perfectly equipped for both entertaining and everyday family life. A range of sleek white high and low Scavolini cabinetry provides ample storage while maintaining a clean aesthetic. There is a handy utility room, ideal for storing everyday essentials and laundry.

The inviting reception room is framed by dual sash windows and features a period fireplace, and retro bare metal radiators (which are throughout the property), there are stripped wooden doors, creating a cosy yet sophisticated ambiance. Meanwhile, the entrance hall sets the tone with its original period terrazzo flooring - a nod to the home's interesting heritage.

Externally, a charming sunny walled patio provides a second entrance to the property and includes a brick-built storage unit, offering practicality with a touch of old-world appeal. This is an ideal place for access and storage of bikes, due to the external gate.

This exceptional home is a rare-find, timeless, unique, and crafted with great care and attention to detail.

There are local shops close by and it is a short stroll away from all the many and varied attractions of Stoke Newington Church Street with its boutique shops, cafes, restaurants and pubs as well as Clissold Park and Abney Park Cemetery. Springfield Park and the local amenities of Stamford Hill are close by and trendy Dalston and up and coming Clapton are also within easy reach.

Located close to Stoke Newington Church Street, it is well located for excellent transport into the City and West End. Stoke Newington Station is close by providing a fast and frequent service into Liverpool Street in one direction and Seven Sisters for the Victoria Line in the other. There are numerous bus routes and Finsbury Park Station is accessible via the 106 bus, and Manor House Tube from the 253 & 254 buses.
The home is also a short walk away from Clapton Station.

19013384.description:
This three-bedroom Victorian home on Lansdowne Drive occupies a premier position directly overlooking London Fields. Arranged over three floors and spanning over 1,550 sqft, the interior is defined by its generous volumes, period details, and a neutral palette that allows the park views to take center stage.

Entry is at upper ground level, opening into a double reception room that immediately establishes the home’s sense of scale. The front room is flooded with light through a large sash window, highlighting the generous ceiling heights and intricate plasterwork. The space is anchored by two marble fireplaces with cast iron inserts, while exposed pine floorboards run underfoot, adding a natural texture to the rooms. A third bedroom is also located on this level, tucked quietly at the rear and with charming views onto the private garden and London Fields.

The lower ground floor functions as the primary entertaining space. It features a large, open-plan living and dining area with a decorative fireplace, leading through to a bright galley style kitchen at the rear, with dark stone worktops, a gas hob, and practical breakfast bar with a window over the garden. A timber-framed glass door opens out to the private garden, which has been thoughtfully landscaped with timber sleepers and a stone-paved terrace. Whilst presented in good decorative order, the house also presents an opportunity to create an infill extension on the lower ground floor, whilst still leaving a good size garden (subject to the usual consents).

On the first floor, the primary bedroom spans the full width of the house with two large sash windows, a cast iron fireplace with marble surround and exposed timber floorboards and ample room for storage. A second double bedroom sits behind it, featuring its own fireplace and built in cabinetry. There is a spacious family bathroom, finished with clean white tiling, a freestanding bathtub, and a separate glass-enclosed shower, as well as an additional guest WC on the lower ground floor.

A new owner will benefit from recent improvements to the exterior of the building, including a new roof to the front of the house in 2022 with new guttering, soffit and fascia boards, as well as repairs to the rear roof in 2025/26 with the addition of new slate tiles where needed, new coping stones, new flashings and arears of repointing to the chimney.

Area

Lansdowne Drive is one of Hackney’s most sought-after addresses, running along the edge of London Fields. The park itself offers fifty acres of green space, including a wildflower meadow, tennis courts, and the famous Olympic-sized Lido.

The house is just moments from Broadway Market, where the street comes alive on Saturdays with independent vendors, alongside permanent fixtures like L'Eau à la Bouche, The Cat & Mutton, Pavilion Bakery, Hill & Szrok, and Donlon Books. Nearby Wilton Way offers an equally local feel with independent cafes and boutiques.

For transport, London Fields station is moments away, providing a direct link to Liverpool Street in approximately 7 minutes, while Hackney Central offers Overground connections across North and East London.

EPC: Band D

A substantial four double bedroom, two bathroom Victorian Freehold with accommodation totalling c. 1,787 sqft, set on a peaceful residential street only moments from Newington Green & Clissold Park.

The room is bathed in natural light, thanks to its large bay window, and boasts beautiful period detailing, including ornate coving, a ceiling rose, and a stunning marble fireplace. At the rear of the ground floor is a generous kitchen/dining area, which provides access to a private courtyard garden through elegant French doors. A large cellar, currently used as a utility space, is also accessible from this level, offering ample storage or further potential for conversion. On the first floor, the principal suite is located at the front of the property, enjoying abundant sunlight through a large bay window, making it a bright and inviting space. The room features a beautiful marble fireplace and built-in wardrobes, adding both character and functionality. A further double bedroom is positioned behind, also benefiting from a fireplace and built-in storage. The bathroom suite, located on the half landing between the ground and first floors, includes a separate walk-in shower for added convenience. The top floor houses two additional double bedrooms, both with fireplaces and built-in wardrobes, providing ample space for family, guests, or home offices. The west-facing rear garden enjoys a wonderfully open and sunny aspect, also benefiting from additional light from the south. It is a peaceful and secluded space, filled with birdsong and mature planting. The garden also features a variety of productive fruit trees and bushes, including damson, greengage, apple, blackberry and loganberry, creating a unique and tranquil setting rarely found so close to the heart of Stoke Newington.

The property also benefits from a cellar, offering useful storage space and excellent potential to convert the basement, subject to the necessary permissions. There remains further scope to extend with a side return extension, and neighbouring properties have already completed similar works, with a party wall already in place to the side.

Dynevor Road is widely regarded as one of Stoke Newington’s most desirable residential streets, just a short walk from the vibrant mix of independent shops, cafés, bars and restaurants along Church Street and Stoke Newington High Street. The excellent William Patten Primary School is nearby, while Abney Park Cemetery and the award-winning Clissold Park are both within easy reach. Transport connections into the City and Central London are convenient, with Stoke Newington Overground Station close by and numerous bus routes along Church Street and the High Street.

19055660.description:
GUIDE PRICE £1,750,000-£2,000,000

Set on the highly sought-after Downham Road, this exceptional four bedroom, four bathroom period residence offers an elegant blend of timeless architecture and refined contemporary living. Beautifully enhanced over many years of careful ownership, the home delivers generous proportions, sophisticated finishes and a calm, residential setting that feels both private and connected.

Having been owned and meticulously maintained by the current owner for 14 years, the property has been thoughtfully upgraded with quality and longevity at the forefront. At the heart of the home is a stunning kitchen installed just two years ago, finished with German granite worktops, a boiling water tap and a striking Rangemaster range cooker with matching hood, creating an impressive space perfectly suited to both everyday living and entertaining.

The lower ground floor benefits from underfloor heating, providing warmth and comfort, while rich Tobacco Oak flooring flows seamlessly throughout the home, enhancing the sense of continuity and craftsmanship. The attention to detail is evident throughout, with wiring and pipework carefully concealed to preserve clean lines and a polished finish.

Further practical improvements elevate the home’s functionality, including a modern boiler installed three years ago, a recently installed water pump delivering excellent water pressure across all bathrooms, and double glazing fitted throughout most of the property with a long-term guarantee, ensuring both comfort and efficiency.

To the rear, a versatile garden shed equipped with both lighting and heating offers additional flexibility, whether used as a studio, workspace or simply a well-appointed storage area.

And then, there is the principal bedroom, a truly standout feature that captures immediate attention, widely considered one of the most romantic bedrooms in De Beauvoir. A beautifully designed master suite where a freestanding bathtub sits elegantly within the room itself, creating a boutique hotel feel that is both indulgent and deeply intimate. This is a space designed not just for rest, but for experience, imagine unwinding in the bath within your own private sanctuary, seamlessly blending comfort, luxury and atmosphere in a way rarely found in London homes.

Combining elegant period character with carefully considered modern upgrades, this is a home designed for refined city living. The generous four bedroom, four bathroom layout offers exceptional flexibility for family life, hosting guests or working from home, all within a beautifully finished and thoughtfully arranged interior.

Opportunities to acquire a home of this calibre on Downham Road are rare. Early viewing is highly recommended to fully appreciate the space, craftsmanship and lifestyle this outstanding residence provides.

19058760.description:

The heart of the home is on the first floor. A spacious reception room offers great space, while the separate kitchen and dining area creates a warm, sociable atmosphere. Large windows ensure every corner is bathed in natural light.

On the top floor, there are two well-appointed bedrooms. The principal bedroom enjoys an en-suite bathroom complete with a bathtub, while the second bedroom benefits from use of a bathroom conveniently positioned off the landing.

Mews parking permits are allocated with the house - these are purchased on an annual permit and provide access to the private mews. The house includes a private garage.


Highbury terrace mews is a sought after location, nestled close to Highbury Fields. Highbury and Islington station is just 0.4 miles from the house, with Victoria line underground links, Overground and National rail links.
The beginning of Upper street is equally 0.4 miles away; Islington's hub for boutique shops, restaurants and cafes.

19088613.description:
This exquisite Victorian terraced house in Stoke Newington has been finished to a beautiful standard throughout. It has four sublime bedrooms on its upper two floors, while its ground level is dedicated to a set of elegant living spaces that flow out towards a private rear garden. Situated on Londesborough Road, the home is a short walk away from the ever-popular Newington Green, Stoke Newington Church Street and restorative Clissold Park.



The Tour



A rose-pink original front door opens to a long hallway with tall ceilings that traverses the ground-floor plan. Rationally apportioned, the home balanced open, convivial spaces with more intimate ones.



A recent renovation has seen original sash windows double-glazed, solid oak floors laid throughout, new cast-iron and joinery fitted, and a new kitchen and bathroom. Farrow and Ball paints have been expertly applied throughout.



At the front of the ground floor is the living room, which has a wide bay with working shutters. A working fireplace lies on one side, framed by its original surround. There are plenty of bookshelves here and in the adjoining dining room; both are grounded with rich, herringbone-laid oak.



The kitchen is at the rear, with a free-standing Britannia range and a Fisher & Paykel larder fridge/freezer. Black cabinetry is topped with quartz counters, and there are large bifold doors that reveal the garden - an arrangement that comes into its own during the spring and summer. Another door provides access to the basement.



A beautiful staircase with a woollen runner curves through the core of the house. There are two bedrooms on the first floor, the larger of which has two sash windows and fitted wardrobes. The rear room is used as a nursery and has more fitted-storage, an original cast-iron fireplace and a delightful hand-painted mural.



There are two more bedrooms upstairs, one with fitted wardrobes and two sash windows, and a smaller, airy space used as a study, with a striking emerald-tiled hearth beneath an original cast-iron fireplace.



The family bathroom is on the first floor and has a crisp finish, with a double-ended slipper bath, a walk-in shower, a ceramic terrazzo tiled floor and brick-shaped wall tiles. Next door is a separate, additional WC; opposite is a laundry zone with stacked white goods.



Outdoor Space



Designed with the same creative ethos as the house itself, the garden is a charming spot. Large tiles provide plenty of space to arrange an outdoor dining set-up, and mature plantings - including fatsia, Italian laurel, magnolia tree, jasmine and Japanese anemone - establish a secret garden-like feel.



The Area



Newington Green is a short walk away and is home to some of north London’s best-loved restaurants, including Jolene, Perilla and Cadet. Local favourite Esters is close by, and there are some noteworthy pubs near the house, including The Shakespeare and The Prince.



On Stoke Newington Church Street, local favourites include The Rose and Crown, Rasa and The Good Egg, along with the wonderful Spence bakery. From here, Clissold Park is easily reached, and is home to tennis courts, ornamental ponds and a family of deer, as well as Fink’s Pumphouse and a new Fink's café in Clissold House.



Canonbury, De Beauvoir, and Upper Street in Islington are also accessible and offer an additional selection of independent boutiques, places to eat, live music, and theatre venues, including The Almedia and Everyman Screen on the Green.



There are some excellent schools locally, including Betty Layward and Stoke Newington School and Sixth Form. Private options in the City are easily accessible by bus. Dalston Kingsland, Canonbury, and Rectory Road stations are all within easy reach and run Overground services on the Mildmay, Windrush and Weaver lines. There are also plenty of stellar bus routes nearby.

Nestled on one of Stoke Newington’s most in demand, tree-lined roads, this spacious five-bedroom, five-bathroom home has been meticulously refurbished with the guidance of an interior designer, achieving an impeccable blend of period charm and contemporary luxury, complete with high ceilings and a stunning three tiered south facing garden. Spanning approximately 2,550 sq ft across four stunning floors, this exceptional home offers both elegance and versatility, making it ideal for a wide range of buyers.



Comprising five double bedrooms, five bathrooms, a 34ft long, dual aspect living room, large eat-in kitchen diner, wide and long south facing garden, low maintenance front garden, utility room and masses of storage.

The upper ground floor is dedicated to a spectacular 34ft dual-aspect reception room, bathed in natural light from a striking bay window at the front and bi-fold doors to the rear, which open onto a Juliet balcony overlooking the garden. This room, with its 10ft high ceilings, two matching marble feature fireplaces, ceiling roses, cornicings and inviting oak herringbone flooring offers an exquisite space for both entertaining and relaxation. A tiled hallway leads to a cloakroom and laundry cupboard, ensuring practicality with style.

Descending to the lower ground floor, a second entrance from the street leads into the heart of the home—a breath taking open-plan kitchen, dining, and living area. Expansive cornered bi-fold doors connect seamlessly to the landscaped garden, creating an exceptional indoor-outdoor flow ripe to entertain or for kids to play whilst cooking. The designer kitchen features navy cabinetry with brass handles, granite worktops, Rangemaster cooker with extractor, integrated Neff appliances, herringbone oak flooring and a variety of mood lighting.

To the front of the property there is a fifth bedroom with an en-suite, making for the perfect guest suite, home office, or au pair accommodation.


Across the first and second floors, this home offers four generous double bedrooms, all beautifully finished with luxury wool carpets, period features, and an abundance of natural light.

The master suite spans the extremely wide width of the property and is complete with a stunning en-suite shower room with a gorgeous blend of gold and matt black fixtures and fittings. The second bedroom on this level enjoys a peaceful rear aspect and easy access to a stylishly finished shower room on the half-landing.

The second floor is home to two further spacious double bedrooms, each offering plenty of space and natural light, along with a contemporary family bathroom. At the very top of the house, a service room discreetly houses the boiler, Megaflow system, and additional storage, ensuring the home runs efficiently without compromising on space.

The 68ft x 20ft south facing garden has been thoughtfully designed to provide a perfect balance of greenery and practicality. A tiled patio area directly off the kitchen/dining space is ideal for outdoor seating and entertaining, while the central lawn and multiple seating areas create a tranquil retreat. With mature planting and well-defined levels, this outdoor space is an extension of the home, perfect for summer gatherings or quiet relaxation. The front garden is equally well-maintained, enhancing the home’s kerb appeal.



This gorgeous home further benefits from masses of kerb appeal, Farrow & Ball paint, luxury wool carpets, new heating system with nest controls, ornate detailing, mood lighting and a gorgeous brick facade.



Brilliantly located for a variety of buyers, Stoke Newington offers a variety of well-loved independent and chain stores, coffee shops, restaurants, bars and more. Dalston is also within walking distance, offering an extensive variety of amenities within walking distance.

Rectory Road is the nearest station with excellent transport links throughout London such as Liverpool Street in under 15 minutes. Dalston Kingsland offers Underground access, with trains towards Stratford and Clapham.

Stoke Newington, known to be a great place for families, has Clissold Park and Hackney Downs within walking distance offering children’s play parks, walking routes, sports courts, nature trails, cafes and open green space.

The property is within the catchment area of the ‘Outstanding’ Ofsted rated Shacklewell Primary School which is just a couple of minutes’ walk away, whilst there are multiple good secondary schools within close proximity.

All the above make this a perfect property and location for an array of buyers.

19079343.description:
Set along one of East London’s most desirable stretches, this beautifully presented Victorian terraced home resides on Victoria Park Road, overlooking Victoria Park itself. Behind the elegant, bay windowed, facade, the house has been carefully reworked to create a well-balanced layout across approximately 1,260 sqft (approx.). Offering three good sized bedrooms and two modern bathrooms, all finished in a clean, understated style the house is well suited to young families and working professionals.

The main living space has been opened up to form a functional, open-plan reception and kitchen area. A subtle split-level design introduces a raised kitchen and dining space, giving a natural sense of separation between cooking and living spaces without losing the overall flow of the room. Designed as much for everyday use as it is for hosting the kitchen itself is both practical and well-resolved, with plenty of workspace, a central island and much needed storage. Double doors draw in soft natural light and open directly onto a private, landscaped courtyard garden. The first of two modern bathrooms as well as the third bedroom make up the remainder of the ground floor. Upstairs you will find two well-proportioned double bedrooms as well as a large family bathroom, complete with freestanding bath and separate shower.

The setting is, without question, one of the home’s defining qualities. Just moments away lies Victoria Park Village (where coffee at Pophams Bakery or lunch in Pub on the Park is a staple), a much-loved enclave known for its independent cafés and neighbourhood delis. A short stroll brings you to Broadway Market, where a dynamic mix of food, drink and local makers creates a distinctly East London energy, while nearby Hackney Wick continues to evolve as one of the capital’s most creative and culturally rich quarters.

19115156.description:
This bay fronted home sits at the end of a small terrace of similar properties leading to Barnsbury Square.

The house is arranged over four floors and offers flexible use of the space. Of note, is the unusual open plan and galleried reception, dining room and office area across the ground floor level. To the rear is a large eat-in kitchen with tiled floor and door leading out to the bright, West Facing garden. Four double bedrooms and the bathroom are arranged over the first and second floor. The large open plan principal bedroom at the top of the house has double doors that lead out to a roof terrace with fabulous views across London.
At the bottom of the house is a large utility room, pantry and vault all offering plenty of extra storage space.

There are plenty of original fixtures and fittings, including fireplaces and original wood floor. Importantly, the house is not listed so, subject to the usual approvals, there is the possibility to extend and convert.

Barnsbury Terrace is in the heart of The Barnsbury Conservation Area, to the west of Upper Street and Liverpool Road. It offers easy access to Angel or Highbury and Islington underground stations and Barnsbury Overground station.
Upper Street is a bustling and vibrant high street with many fabulous shops, bars, and bistros. We have the great Almeida Theatre, King’s head Theatre and Sadlers Wells in addition to the delightful Little Angel Theatre amongst many others. The Albion with its large garden, The Drapers Arms and The Tamil Prince offer award winning food and drinks. King’s Cross is also close by with the fabulous Granary Square, Coal Drops Yard and modern restaurants, whilst not forgetting the international hub for rapid transport to Europe.

19103509.description:
A charming period house arranged over three floors, enviably positioned on the sought-after Sudeley Street, N1.
The ground floor is greeted by a beautifully proportioned double reception room, rich in period character and featuring bespoke built-in storage, creating an elegant yet practical living space. On this level, the house opens onto a delightful private garden, ideal for both entertaining and everyday enjoyment.
The lower ground floor hosts a well-appointed kitchen alongside an additional reception room, offering excellent versatility as a dining room, snug, or home office. This level presents exciting potential to extend, subject to the necessary planning consents, allowing the incoming owner to further enhance and personalise the accommodation.
On the first floor, a large double bedroom is filled with charm and natural light, complemented by a generous family bathroom. The top floor provides a second double bedroom, completing the accommodation.
features: Period property set over three floors

19099540.description:
This exceptional Grade II listed Georgian house occupies a prime position on one of Canonbury’s most sought-after streets and offers approximately 1,840 sq ft of beautifully proportioned accommodation arranged over multiple floors. The house retains a wealth of original period features, including tall sash windows with original shutters, ornate cornicing and feature fireplaces, creating elegant and light-filled rooms typical of Georgian architecture.

The ground floor provides a series of impressive reception spaces with excellent ceiling heights and classical proportions, ideal for both family living and entertaining. The lower ground level hosts the kitchen and dining areas with a direct outlook to the garden, forming the heart of the home.

Upstairs, the accommodation comprises three generous double bedrooms arranged across the upper floors, together with well-appointed bathroom facilities. The principal bedroom and bathroom are set on the first floor and boast wonderful natural light whilst retaining a strong sense of character throughout. The second floor of the property holds the other two double bedrooms.

To the rear is a substantial south-west facing garden extending to over 45 feet, offering a rare sense of privacy and sunshine in such a central location and providing an ideal setting for outdoor dining and relaxation.

The house also benefits from dry-lined vaults providing valuable additional storage, further enhancing its practicality as a long-term family home.

Positioned within the William Tyndale Primary School catchment area, and moments from Upper Street, Highbury Fields and Highbury & Islington station, this is a rare opportunity to acquire a distinguished Georgian home in one of Islington’s most desirable residential settings.
features: Full of Character and Original Features

19104105.description:
This charming three-bedroom home is set on a beautifully quiet residential street in the heart of Hackney and offers 1,350 sqft of characterful living space. Rich in period features and natural charm, the property provides a wonderful balance of warmth, space and calm.

The accommodation is arranged over multiple levels and includes generous reception areas ideal for both family living and entertaining. The kitchen and dining space offers a practical layout, while the first floor provide three well-proportioned bedrooms alongside a family bathroom. Throughout the house, original detailing and attractive proportions create a welcoming and homely atmosphere.

This is a rare opportunity to acquire a substantial period property in a prime Hackney location, combining immediate comfort.

19104406.description:
Built in 2016, this contemporary freehold townhouse on Compton Avenue offers a rare opportunity to acquire a modern home in one of Canonbury’s most sought-after locations. Set over four thoughtfully designed floors, the property combines generous proportions with a refined finish and exceptional natural light throughout.
On entry, a bright reception room with floor-to-ceiling windows provides an immediate sense of openness and light, with a clear view through to the green outlook at the rear. This level also includes a well-proportioned study, ideal for home working or use as a quiet reading space.
The lower ground floor is designed for modern living and entertaining, featuring a spacious open-plan kitchen and dining area with doors opening directly onto a private patio. Underfloor heating runs throughout, while integrated speakers enhance the atmosphere for relaxed dining or social gatherings.
The first floor provides two double bedrooms, one with en-suite, with fitted storage and a stylish family bathroom. The principal suite occupies the top floor, offering ample wardrobe space, a sleek en suite shower room, and direct access to a private roof terrace with open views towards the Union Chapel, creating a peaceful setting for morning coffee or evening relaxation.
Residents also benefit from a concierge service and a secure bike store, adding convenience and security to this exceptional home.
With its prime Canonbury location, striking modern design, and superb specification, this townhouse is perfectly placed for Highbury & Islington Station, Upper Street’s vibrant selection of shops, cafés and restaurants, and nearby green spaces.
features: Moments from Highbury and Islington station

18600206.description:
This charming Victorian house offers beautifully proportioned family accommodation arranged over two storeys, extending to approximately 954 sq ft, with excellent potential to extend, subject to the usual planning consents. The property is being offered on a chain free basis.
Retaining a wealth of period charm, the property features original Victorian fireplaces, high ceilings, and elegant proportions, seamlessly combined with modern finishes. The ground floor comprises a welcoming reception hall with useful understairs storage, twin reception rooms, a family bathroom, and a well appointed fitted kitchen with appliances and doors opening onto a secluded rear garden, perfect for family life and entertaining.
Upstairs, there are three generous bedrooms, providing flexible accommodation for families.
Oriel Road has a quiet, village like feel, close to Victoria Park with its playgrounds, sports pitches, and scenic walks, as well as other nearby green spaces including London Fields and Millfields Park. There are also a number of well-regarded local schools nearby, making this an ideal location for families. Local dining is excellent, with Lardo and Raw Duck near London Fields, and Casa Fofo and The Grand Howl on Well Street for coffee.
Chatsworth Road offers independent shops, cafés, and restaurants, while Victoria Park Village features top-quality food shops like The Ginger Pig and fishmonger Jonathan Norris. Weekly markets take place at Victoria Park and Well Street, with Broadway Market hosting a popular Saturday food market.
Transport links are excellent. Homerton Overground Station is just a two-minute walk, providing direct services to Stratford and Highbury & Islington. London Fields Station offers direct trains to Liverpool Street, and Bethnal Green Underground Station, easily accessible by bus, gives fast access into central London via the Central Line.
features: Charming Victorian house, being offered chain-free.
Original fireplaces, high ceilings, period charm.
Reception hall and twin reception rooms.
Quiet Oriel Road, near Victoria Park
Independent shops, cafés, and weekly markets nearby.
Within walking distance of Homerton overground station

19103280.description:
Set across four beautifully arranged floors and offering close to 1,700 sq ft of internal space, this elegant period home blends classic charm with generous proportions throughout.
As you enter the raised ground floor, you're welcomed by a stunning double reception room, complete with period features including high ceilings, sash windows, and original fireplaces—ideal for both entertaining and relaxed living.
The lower ground level boasts a spacious, well-appointed kitchen and a separate formal dining room with French doors leading directly onto a private, landscaped garden—perfect for al fresco dining and entertaining.
Upstairs, the first floor offers two comfortable double bedrooms and a stylish family bathroom, while the top floor is dedicated to a superb principal suite. This includes a large dressing area and a luxurious en suite bathroom, creating a true retreat.
features: Excellent location. Set over four floors

19102769.description:
This exceptional three-bedroom period house offers over 1,900 sq ft of beautifully designed living space, blending timeless character with high-spec contemporary finishes throughout. Arranged across multiple levels, the property provides generous proportions, excellent storage and a rare level of modern convenience within one of De Beauvoir’s most sought-after residential settings.

The ground floor is centred around a stunning open-plan kitchen and living space, thoughtfully extended to create a bright and sociable heart of the home. Large doors open directly onto the south-east facing garden, allowing natural light to flood the space and providing an ideal setting for both entertaining and day-to-day family living. The kitchen and reception areas also benefit from a fully integrated SONOS ceiling speaker system and a Rako lighting system, offering seamless smart control and a sophisticated atmosphere.

Upstairs, three spacious bedrooms are arranged across the upper floors alongside well-appointed bathroom facilities, all retaining attractive period features and excellent natural light. The remainder of the house (excluding the office) is fitted with a Philips Hue lighting system, allowing flexible and customisable smart lighting throughout.

A generous cellar level provides substantial additional storage and further versatility, enhancing the practicality of this impressive home.

13640650.description:
Guide Price £2.000,000 - £2.150,000. Situated on one of Stoke Newington's premium roads, comes this exceptional residence providing five bedrooms and four bathrooms measuring in excess of 2300 Sq ft which has been lovingly refurbished with the advice from an interior designer. OFFERED CHAIN FREE.

The property is generously proportioned throughout whilst the ceiling height is additionally generous, adding to its overall volume and allowing rooms to bathe in natural light. The accommodation offers flexibility so will cater for many people with diverse usage. Currently comprising on the upper ground floor - superb living accommodation, with a formal reception room which is 34ft in length and runs the depth of the house, with dual aspect windows, making the room illuminate and offers an ideal entertaining space. Large bay window to the front elevation and the rear elevation has bi-fold doors opening onto a Juliet balcony overlooking the garden. This room has two feature fireplaces and is paired with a striking, warm oak herringbone engineered wood floor. The tiled hallway floor leads to a cloakroom and laundry cupboard.

Carpeted stairs lead down to the lower floor of the house which has its own entrance door from the street. The hallway leads into the open plan kitchen, dining and living space with windows to the front elevation and bi-fold doors that open onto the garden patio. This herringbone oak wooden floor runs throughout this area with under floor heating. The kitchen has smart navy cabinets with brass handles and is paired with beautiful marble-veined granite work tops.
There is a Rangemaster cooker and extractor and fully integrated Neff appliances. On this floor you will also find the fifth bedroom which has its own En-suite and acts as an ideal guest room.

The first floor provides two of the bedrooms, with the master running the full width across the front of the house and this room accesses a chic En-suite, whilst to the rear of this floor the second bedroom looks over the landscaped garden. There is a shower room on the half landing between the first and second floors.

The second floor (top) provides the third and fourth bedrooms. The third bedroom to the front elevation and the fourth bedroom looking out over the rear garden. There is the family bathroom on this floor. Stairs lead up to the service room which houses the gas boiler for the central heating and the Megaflow water cylinders that supplies the hot water for the house.
The TV aerial and booster is also in this room.

Farrow and Ball paint has been used through out the house and the wool carpets are of natural shades to compliment the paint work in each room.

The plot is an outstanding size and offers a small, low maintenance garden to the front, and a large rear garden which measures approximately 62ft in depth, this is mainly laid to lawn and has three raised areas, with the first being off of a neat patio area which sits outside the kitchen/diner, with a small raised area at the most rear of the garden.

Farleigh Road is well placed close to all the local amenities and transport links that the area brings and is only a half mile from Stoke Newington's Church Street opening up its plethora of trendy Bars and Restaurants. The iconic landmarks of The Arcola Theatre and the Rio Cinema are also located nearby along with other amenities and transport links being offered by neighbouring Dalston.

16200943.description:
Douglas Allen has the pleasure of offering this 5/6 bedroom rarely available Period property located one of Stamford Hill's most sought-after streets, Durley Road. The house is set over numerous levels.

The property, spread over four floors, retains numerous period features with an opulent staircase being one of its charms. This is a dream home for those looking to settle in this safe and welcoming community.

The lower ground floor is a self-contained one-bedroom flat with its own entrance, bathroom, reception, a separate kitchen. The first and second floor consist of five bedrooms with a family bathroom, kitchen reception room and additional toilet. At the rear of the property the modern kitchen leads out to the private landscape garden.

This is a rare and excellent opportunity to purchase such a large and unique property. Durley Road is a quiet, tree lined residential street located moments away from the local shops and amenities of Stamford Hill. Excellent transport links into the City and West End, with lots of bus routes, Stoke Newington and Stamford Hill Overground stations offering a fast and frequent service into Liverpool Street and Seven Sisters. Manor House tube station (Piccadilly line) is moments away and takes you to Heathrow airport, as well as only being a short walk from the stunning Springfield Park which is famous for its landscapes, canal boats and tennis courts.
